NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy my brother,—
text.KJV: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
text.DRB: Paul an Apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and brother Timothee:

text.NRSV: To the saints and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ in Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
text.Rotherham: Unto the holy and faithful brethren in Christ that are, in Colosse, favour unto you, and peace, from our God and Father.
text.KJV: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: to the holy ones and faithful brothers in Christ in Colossae: grace to you and peace from God our Father. Thanksgiving.
text.DRB: to them that are at Colossa saints and faithful brethren in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: In our prayers for you we always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.Rotherham: We are giving thanks unto God, the Father of our Lord Jesus [Christ], always, for you, offering prayer,—
text.KJV: We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
text.NABRE: We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
text.DRB: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ. We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ always for you, praying:

text.NRSV: for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love that you have for all the saints,
text.Rotherham: Having heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love which ye have unto all the saints,—
text.KJV: Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which ye have to all the saints,
text.NABRE: for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and the love that you have for all the holy ones
text.DRB: hearing your faith in Christ Jesus, and the love which you have toward all the saints,

text.NRSV: because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. You have heard of this hope before in the word of the truth, the gospel
text.Rotherham: Because of the hope that is lying by for you in the heavens, of which ye heard before, in the word of the truth of the glad-message,
text.KJV: For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;
text.NABRE: because of the hope reserved for you in heaven. Of this you have already heard through the word of truth, the gospel,
text.DRB: for the hope that is laid up for you in Heaven, which you have heard in the word of the truth of the Gospel,

text.NRSV: that has come to you. Just as it is bearing fruit and growing in the whole world, so it has been bearing fruit among yourselves from the day you heard it and truly comprehended the grace of God.
text.Rotherham: When it presented itself unto you; even as, in all the world also, it is bearing fruit and growing, even as also among you, from the day when ye heard, and came personally to know the favour of God in truth,
text.KJV: Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:
text.NABRE: that has come to you. Just as in the whole world it is bearing fruit and growing, so also among you, from the day you heard it and came to know the grace of God in truth,
text.DRB: that is come to you, as also in the whole world it is, and fructifieth, and groweth, even as in you, since that day that you heard and knew the grace of God in truth,

text.NRSV: This you learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant. He is a faithful minister of Christ on your behalf,
text.Rotherham: Even as ye learned [it] from Epaphras, our beloved fellow-servant, who is faithful in our behalf, as a minister of the Christ,
text.KJV: As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;
text.NABRE: as you learned it from Epaphras our beloved fellow slave, who is a trustworthy minister of Christ on your behalf
text.DRB: as you learned of Epaphras our dearest fellow-servant, who is a faithful minister of Jesus Christ for you,

text.NRSV: and he has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Who also hath made evident unto us your love in spirit.
text.KJV: Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.
text.NABRE: and who also told us of your love in the Spirit. Prayer for Continued Progress.
text.DRB: who also hath manifested to us your love in spirit.

text.NRSV: For this reason, since the day we heard it, we have not ceased praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of God’s will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, we also, from the day when we heard [of you], cease not, in your behalf, praying and asking—that ye may be filled unto the personal knowledge of his will, in all spiritual wisdom and discernment,
text.KJV: For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;
text.NABRE: Therefore, from the day we heard this, we do not cease praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will through all spiritual wisdom and understanding
text.DRB: Therefore we also from the day that we heard it, cease not praying for you and desiring, that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will, in all wisdom, and spiritual understanding:

text.NRSV: so that you may lead a life worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to him, as you bear fruit in every good work and as you grow in the knowledge of God.
text.Rotherham: So as to walk worthily of the Lord, unto all pleasing, in every good work, bearing fruit, and growing in the personal knowledge of God,
text.KJV: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;
text.NABRE: to live in a manner worthy of the Lord, so as to be fully pleasing, in every good work bearing fruit and growing in the knowledge of God,
text.DRB: that you may walk worthy of God, in all things pleasing: fructifying in all good work, & increasing in the knowledge of God:

text.NRSV: May you be made strong with all the strength that comes from his glorious power, and may you be prepared to endure everything with patience, while joyfully
text.Rotherham: With all power, being empowered, according to the grasp of his glory, unto all endurance and long-suffering with joy,
text.KJV: Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;
text.NABRE: strengthened with every power, in accord with his glorious might, for all endurance and patience, with joy
text.DRB: in all power strengthened according to the might of his glory, in all patience and longanimity with joy

text.NRSV: giving thanks to the Father, who has enabled you to share in the inheritance of the saints in the light.
text.Rotherham: Giving thanks unto the Father that hath made you sufficient for your share in the inheritance of the saints in the light,
text.KJV: Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:
text.NABRE: giving thanks to the Father, who has made you fit to share in the inheritance of the holy ones in light.
text.DRB: giving thanks to God and the Father, who hath made us worthy unto the part of the lot of the saints in the light:

text.NRSV: He has rescued us from the power of darkness and transferred us into the kingdom of his beloved Son,
text.Rotherham: Who hath rescued us out of the authority of the darkness, and translated [us] into the kingdom of the Son of his love;
text.KJV: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear Son:
text.NABRE: He delivered us from the power of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of his beloved Son,
text.DRB: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the Kingdom of the Son of his love,

text.NRSV: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
text.Rotherham: In whom, we have our redemption—the remission of our sins,—
text.KJV: In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins:
text.NABRE: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
text.DRB: in whom we have redemption, the remission of sins:

text.NRSV: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation;
text.Rotherham: Who, is an image of the unseen God, Firstborn of all creation,—
text.KJV: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:
text.NABRE: II. The Preeminence of Christ His Person and Work He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.
text.DRB: who is the image of the invisible God, the first-born of all creature:

text.NRSV: for in him all things in heaven and on earth were created, things visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or powers—all things have been created through him and for him.
text.Rotherham: Because, in him, were created all things in the heavens and upon the earth, the things seen and the things unseen, whether thrones or lordships or principalities or authorities,—they all, through him and for him, have been created,
text.KJV: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:
text.NABRE: For in him were created all things in heaven and on earth, the visible and the invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things were created through him and for him.
text.DRB: because in him were created all things in Heaven, and in earth, visible, and invisible, whether Thrones, or Dominations, or Principalities, or Potestates: all by him, & in him were created:

text.NRSV: He himself is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
text.Rotherham: And, he, is before all, and, they all, in him, hold together;
text.KJV: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
text.NABRE: He is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
text.DRB: and he is before all, and all consist in him.

text.NRSV: He is the head of the body, the church; he is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that he might come to have first place in everything.
text.Rotherham: And, he, is the head of the body, the assembly, Who is the beginning, Firstborn from among the dead, in order that, he, might become, in all things, himself, pre-eminent;—
text.KJV: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.
text.NABRE: He is the head of the body, the church. He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in all things he himself might be preeminent.
text.DRB: And he is the Head of the body, the Church, who is the beginning, first-born of the dead: that he may be in all things holding the primacy:

text.NRSV: For in him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell,
text.Rotherham: Because, in him, was all the fullness well pleased to dwell,
text.KJV: For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;
text.NABRE: For in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell,
text.DRB: because in him it hath well pleased, all fullness to inhabit:

text.NRSV: and through him God was pleased to reconcile to himself all things, whether on earth or in heaven, by making peace through the blood of his cross.
text.Rotherham: And, through him, fully to reconcile all things unto him, making peace through the blood of his cross,— [through him] —whether the things upon the earth or the things in the heavens;
text.KJV: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.
text.NABRE: and through him to reconcile all things for him, making peace by the blood of his cross [through him], whether those on earth or those in heaven.
text.DRB: and by him to reconcile all things unto himself, pacifying by the blood of his cross, whether the things in earth, or the things that are in Heaven.

text.NRSV: And you who were once estranged and hostile in mind, doing evil deeds,
text.Rotherham: And, you, who at one time were estranged and enemies in your mind in your wicked works, yet, now, hath he fully reconciled,
text.KJV: And you, that were sometime alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled
text.NABRE: And you who once were alienated and hostile in mind because of evil deeds
text.DRB: And you, whereas you were sometime alienated and enemies in sense, in evil works:

text.NRSV: he has now reconciled in his fleshy body through death, so as to present you holy and blameless and irreproachable before him—
text.Rotherham: In his body of flesh, through means of his death, to present you holy and blameless and unaccusable before him,—
text.KJV: In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:
text.NABRE: he has now reconciled in his fleshly body through his death, to present you holy, without blemish, and irreproachable before him,
text.DRB: yet now he hath reconciled in the body of his flesh by death, to present you holy & immaculate, and blameless before him:

text.NRSV: provided that you continue securely established and steadfast in the faith, without shifting from the hope promised by the gospel that you heard, which has been proclaimed to every creature under heaven. I, Paul, became a servant of this gospel.
text.Rotherham: If, at least, ye are abiding still in the faith, founded and firm, and not to be moved away from the hope of the glad-message which ye have heard, which hath been proclaimed in all creation which is under heaven,—of which, I Paul, have become minister.
text.KJV: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel, which ye have heard, and which was preached to every creature which is under heaven; whereof I Paul am made a minister;
text.NABRE: provided that you persevere in the faith, firmly grounded, stable, and not shifting from the hope of the gospel that you heard, which has been preached to every creature under heaven, of which I, Paul, am a minister. Christ in Us.
text.DRB: if yet ye continue in the faith, grounded and stable, and unmovable from the hope of the Gospel which you have heard, which is preached among all creatures that are under Heaven, whereof I Paul am made a minister.

text.NRSV: I am now rejoicing in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I am completing what is lacking in Christ’s afflictions for the sake of his body, that is, the church.
text.Rotherham: Now, am I rejoicing in the sufferings on your behalf, and am filling up the things that lack of the tribulations of the Christ, in my flesh, in behalf of his body, which is the assembly,
text.KJV: Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the church:
text.NABRE: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I am filling up what is lacking in the afflictions of Christ on behalf of his body, which is the church,
text.DRB: Who now rejoice in suffering for you, and do accomplish those things that want of the passions of Christ, in my flesh for his body, which is the Church:

text.NRSV: I became its servant according to God’s commission that was given to me for you, to make the word of God fully known,
text.Rotherham: Of which, I, have become minister—according to the administration of God which hath been given unto me to you-ward, to fill up the word of God,
text.KJV: Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;
text.NABRE: of which I am a minister in accordance with God’s stewardship given to me to bring to completion for you the word of God,
text.DRB: whereof I am made a minister according to the dispensation of God, which is given me toward you, that I may fulfill the word of God,

text.NRSV: the mystery that has been kept hidden throughout the ages and generations but has now been revealed to his saints.
text.Rotherham: The sacred secret which had been hidden away from the ages and from the generations, but, now, hath been made manifest unto his saints—
text.KJV: Even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made manifest to his saints:
text.NABRE: the mystery hidden from ages and from generations past. But now it has been manifested to his holy ones,
text.DRB: the mystery that hath been hidden from worlds and generations, but now is manifested to his saints,

text.NRSV: To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom God hath been pleased to make known what is the glorious wealth of this sacred secret among the nations, which is Christ in you, the hope of the glory,—
text.KJV: To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:
text.NABRE: to whom God chose to make known the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; it is Christ in you, the hope for glory.
text.DRB: to whom God would make known the riches of the glory of his sacrament in the Gentiles, which is Christ, in you the hope of glory,

text.NRSV: It is he whom we proclaim, warning everyone and teaching everyone in all wisdom, so that we may present everyone mature in Christ.
text.Rotherham: Whom we are declaring, admonishing every man, and teaching every man, in all wisdom, in order that we may present every man complete in Christ;
text.KJV: Whom we preach, warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:
text.NABRE: It is he whom we proclaim, admonishing everyone and teaching everyone with all wisdom, that we may present everyone perfect in Christ.
text.DRB: whom we preach, admonishing every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: For this I toil and struggle with all the energy that he powerfully inspires within me.
text.Rotherham: Unto which I am even toiling, contending according to his energy which is energising itself in me with power.
text.KJV: Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.
text.NABRE: For this I labor and struggle, in accord with the exercise of his power working within me.
text.DRB: Wherein also I labour striving according to his operation which he worketh in me in power.

text.NRSV: For I want you to know how much I am struggling for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for all who have not seen me face to face.
text.Rotherham: For I desire you to know, how great a contest I am having—in behalf of you, and of those in Laodicea, and as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
text.KJV: For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you, and for them at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 For I want you to know how great a struggle I am having for you and for those in Laodicea and all who have not seen me face to face,
text.DRB: For I will have you know, brethren, what manner of care I have for you and for them that are at Laodicia, and whosoever have not seen my face in the flesh:

text.NRSV: I want their hearts to be encouraged and united in love, so that they may have all the riches of assured understanding and have the knowledge of God’s mystery, that is, Christ himself,
text.Rotherham: In order that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love, even unto all the riches of the full assurance of their understanding, unto a personal knowledge of the sacred secret of God,—Christ:
text.KJV: That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;
text.NABRE: that their hearts may be encouraged as they are brought together in love, to have all the richness of fully assured understanding, for the knowledge of the mystery of God, Christ,
text.DRB: that their hearts may be comforted, instructed in charity, and unto all the riches of the fullness of understanding, unto the knowledge of the mystery of God the Father of Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
text.Rotherham: In whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden away.
text.KJV: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
text.NABRE: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
text.DRB: in whom be all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hid.

text.NRSV: I am saying this so that no one may deceive you with plausible arguments.
text.Rotherham: This I say, in order that, no one, may be reasoning, you, aside with plausible discourse;
text.KJV: And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
text.NABRE: III. Warnings Against False Teachers A General Admonition. I say this so that no one may deceive you by specious arguments.
text.DRB: But this I say that no man deceive you in loftiness of words.

text.NRSV: For though I am absent in body, yet I am with you in spirit, and I rejoice to see your morale and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, though, indeed, in the flesh, I am absent, yet, in the spirit, with you, I am—rejoicing, and beholding your order and the solid firmness of your Christ-ward faith.
text.KJV: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the stedfastness of your faith in Christ.
text.NABRE: For even if I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in spirit, rejoicing as I observe your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
text.DRB: For although I be absent in body, yet in spirit I am with you; rejoicing, and seeing your order, and the constancy of that your faith which is in Christ.

text.NRSV: As you therefore have received Christ Jesus the Lord, continue to live your lives in him,
text.Rotherham: As therefore ye have accepted the Anointed Jesus as your Lord, in him, be walking,—
text.KJV: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:
text.NABRE: So, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, walk in him,
text.DRB: Therefore as you have received Jesus Christ our Lord, walk in him,

text.NRSV: rooted and built up in him and established in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
text.Rotherham: Rooted, and being built up, in him, and making yourselves sure in your faith, even as ye have been taught,—surpassing therein with thanksgiving.
text.KJV: Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.
text.NABRE: rooted in him and built upon him and established in the faith as you were taught, abounding in thanksgiving.
text.DRB: rooted and built in him, and confirmed in the faith, as also you have learned, abounding in him in thanks-giving.

text.NRSV: See to it that no one takes you captive through philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits of the universe, and not according to Christ.
text.Rotherham: Be taking heed, lest there shall be anyone leading, you, off as a spoil, through means of their philosophy, and an empty deceit,—according to the instruction of men, according to the first principles of the world,—and not according to Christ:
text.KJV: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.
text.NABRE: See to it that no one captivate you with an empty, seductive philosophy according to human tradition, according to the elemental powers of the world and not according to Christ.
text.DRB: Beware lest any man deceive you by philosophy, & vain fallacy; according to the tradition of men, according to the elements of the world, and not according to Christ.

text.NRSV: For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily,
text.Rotherham: Because, in him, dwelleth all the fullness of the Godhead, bodily,
text.KJV: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
text.NABRE: Sovereign Role of Christ. For in him dwells the whole fullness of the deity bodily,
text.DRB: For in him dwelleth all the fullness of the Godhead corporally:

text.NRSV: and you have come to fullness in him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.
text.Rotherham: And ye are, in him, filled full,—Who, is the head of all principality and authority,
text.KJV: And ye are complete in him, which is the head of all principality and power:
text.NABRE: and you share in this fullness in him, who is the head of every principality and power.
text.DRB: and you are in him replenished, who is the Head in all principality and power:

text.NRSV: In him also you were circumcised with a spiritual circumcision, by putting off the body of the flesh in the circumcision of Christ;
text.Rotherham: In whom, ye have also been circumcised with a circumcision not done by hand, in the despoiling of the body of flesh, in the circumcision of the Christ,—
text.KJV: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:
text.NABRE: In him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not administered by hand, by stripping off the carnal body, with the circumcision of Christ.
text.DRB: in whom all you are circumcised with circumcision not made by hand in spoiling of the body of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ,

text.NRSV: when you were buried with him in baptism, you were also raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Having been buried together with him in your immersion, wherein also ye have been raised together, through your faith in the energising of God—Who raised him from among the dead.
text.KJV: Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.
text.NABRE: You were buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
text.DRB: buried with him in Baptism: in whom also you are risen again by the faith of the operation of God, who raised him up from the dead.

text.NRSV: And when you were dead in trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh, God made you alive together with him, when he forgave us all our trespasses,
text.Rotherham: And, as for you—who were, dead, by your offences and by the uncircumcision of your flesh, he hath brought you to life together with him,—having in favour forgiven us all our offences,
text.KJV: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;
text.NABRE: And even when you were dead [in] transgressions and the uncircumcision of your flesh, he brought you to life along with him, having forgiven us all our transgressions;
text.DRB: And you when you were dead in the offences and the prepuce of your flesh, did he quicken together with him; pardoning you all offences,

text.NRSV: erasing the record that stood against us with its legal demands. He set this aside, nailing it to the cross.
text.Rotherham: Having blotted out the handwriting against us by the decrees, which was hostile to us,—and hath taken away, the same, out of the midst, nailing it up to the cross:
text.KJV: Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;
text.NABRE: obliterating the bond against us, with its legal claims, which was opposed to us, he also removed it from our midst, nailing it to the cross;
text.DRB: wiping out the hand-writing of decree that was against us, which was contrary to us. And the same he hath taken out of the way, fastening it to the cross:

text.NRSV: He disarmed the rulers and authorities and made a public example of them, triumphing over them in it.
text.Rotherham: Spoiling the principalities and the authorities, he made of them an open example, celebrating a triumph over them thereby.
text.KJV: And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
text.NABRE: despoiling the principalities and the powers, he made a public spectacle of them, leading them away in triumph by it.
text.DRB: and spoiling the principalities & Potestates, hath led them confidently in open shew, triumphing them in himself.

text.NRSV: Therefore do not let anyone condemn you in matters of food and drink or of observing festivals, new moons, or sabbaths.
text.Rotherham: Let no one, therefore, be judging, you,—in eating and in drinking, or in respect of feast, or new moon, or sabbath,—
text.KJV: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:
text.NABRE: Practices Contrary to Faith. Let no one, then, pass judgment on you in matters of food and drink or with regard to a festival or new moon or sabbath.
text.DRB: Let no man therefore judge you in meat or in drink, or in part of a festival day, or of the New-moon, or of Sabboths:

text.NRSV: These are only a shadow of what is to come, but the substance belongs to Christ.
text.Rotherham: Which are a shadow of the things to come, whereas, the body, is of the Christ.
text.KJV: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.
text.NABRE: These are shadows of things to come; the reality belongs to Christ.
text.DRB: which are a shadow of things to come, but the body Christ's.

text.NRSV: Do not let anyone disqualify you, insisting on self-abasement and worship of angels, dwelling on visions, puffed up without cause by a human way of thinking,
text.Rotherham: Let, no one, against you, be arbitrating, however wishful,—in respect of lowliness of mind, and of a religious observance of the messengers: upon what things he hath seen, taking his stand, in vain, puffed up by his carnal mind,—
text.KJV: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
text.NABRE: Let no one disqualify you, delighting in self-abasement and worship of angels, taking his stand on visions, inflated without reason by his fleshly mind,
text.DRB: Let no man seduce you, willing in the humility and religion of Angels, walking in the things which he hath not seen, in vain puffed up by the sense of his flesh,

text.NRSV: and not holding fast to the head, from whom the whole body, nourished and held together by its ligaments and sinews, grows with a growth that is from God.
text.Rotherham: And not holding fast the head: from which, all the body, through means of its joints and uniting bands, receiving supply, and connecting itself together, groweth with the growth of God.
text.KJV: And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.
text.NABRE: and not holding closely to the head, from whom the whole body, supported and held together by its ligaments and bonds, achieves the growth that comes from God.
text.DRB: and not holding the Head, whereof the whole body by joints and bands being served and compacted, groweth to the increase of God.

text.NRSV: If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the universe, why do you live as if you still belonged to the world? Why do you submit to regulations,
text.Rotherham: If ye have died, together with Christ, from the first principles of the world, why, as though alive in the world, are ye submitting to decrees,—
text.KJV: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,
text.NABRE: If you died with Christ to the elemental powers of the world, why do you submit to regulations as if you were still living in the world?
text.DRB: If then you be dead with Christ, from the elements of this world; why do you yet decree as living in the world?

text.NRSV: “Do not handle, Do not taste, Do not touch”?
text.Rotherham: Do not handle, nor taste, nor touch;—
text.KJV: (Touch not; taste not; handle not;
text.NABRE: “Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!”
text.DRB: Touch not, taste not, handle not:

text.NRSV: All these regulations refer to things that perish with use; they are simply human commands and teachings.
text.Rotherham: Which things are all for decay in the using up;—according to the commandments and teachings of men?
text.KJV: Which all are to perish with the using;) after the commandments and doctrines of men?
text.NABRE: These are all things destined to perish with use; they accord with human precepts and teachings.
text.DRB: which things are all unto destruction by the very use, according to the precepts and doctrines of men.

text.NRSV: These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-imposed piety, humility, and severe treatment of the body, but they are of no value in checking self-indulgence.
text.Rotherham: The which things, indeed, though they have, an appearance, of wisdom, in self-devised religious observance, and lowliness of mind, [and] ill-treatment of body, are, in no honourable way, unto a satisfying of the flesh.
text.KJV: Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body; not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.
text.NABRE: While they have a semblance of wisdom in rigor of devotion and self-abasement [and] severity to the body, they are of no value against gratification of the flesh.
text.DRB: Which are indeed having a shew of wisdom in superstition and humility, and not to spare the body, not in any honour to the filling of the flesh.

text.NRSV: So if you have been raised with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, ye have been raised together with the Christ, the things on high, be seeking, where, the Christ, is—on the right hand of God, sitting;
text.KJV: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
text.NABRE: IV. The Ideal Christian Life in the World Chapter 3 Mystical Death and Resurrection. If then you were raised with Christ, seek what is above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God.
text.DRB: Therefore if you be risen with Christ, seek the things that are above; where Christ is sitting on the right hand of God.

text.NRSV: Set your minds on things that are above, not on things that are on earth,
text.Rotherham: The things on high, hold in esteem, not the things upon the earth:
text.KJV: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
text.NABRE: Think of what is above, not of what is on earth.
text.DRB: Mind the things that are above, not the things that are upon the earth.

text.NRSV: for you have died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
text.Rotherham: For ye have died, and, your life, is hid, together with the Christ, in God,—
text.KJV: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.
text.NABRE: For you have died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
text.DRB: For you are dead; and your life is hid with Christ in God.

text.NRSV: When Christ who is your life is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.
text.Rotherham: As soon as, the Christ, shall be made manifest—our life, then, ye also, together with him, shall be made manifest in glory;
text.KJV: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.
text.NABRE: When Christ your life appears, then you too will appear with him in glory. Renunciation of Vice.
text.DRB: When Christ shall appear, your life; then you also shall appear with him in glory.

text.NRSV: Put to death, therefore, whatever in you is earthly: fornication, impurity, passion, evil desire, and greed (which is idolatry).
text.Rotherham: Make dead, therefore, your members that are on the earth—as regardeth fornication, impurity, passion, base coveting, and greed, the which, is idolatry,—
text.KJV: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:
text.NABRE: Put to death, then, the parts of you that are earthly: immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and the greed that is idolatry.
text.DRB: Mortify therefore your members that are upon the earth, fornication, uncleanness, lust, evil concupiscence, and avarice, which is the service of idols.

text.NRSV: On account of these the wrath of God is coming on those who are disobedient.
text.Rotherham: On account of which things cometh the anger of God,—
text.KJV: For which things’ sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:
text.NABRE: Because of these the wrath of God is coming [upon the disobedient].
text.DRB: For which things the wrath of God cometh upon the children of incredulity.

text.NRSV: These are the ways you also once followed, when you were living that life.
text.Rotherham: Wherein, ye also, walked, at one time, when ye were living in these things;
text.KJV: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.
text.NABRE: By these you too once conducted yourselves, when you lived in that way.
text.DRB: In which you also walked sometime, when you lived in them.

text.NRSV: But now you must get rid of all such things—anger, wrath, malice, slander, and abusive language from your mouth.
text.Rotherham: But, now, do, ye also, put them all away,—anger, wrath, baseness, defamation, shameful talk out of your mouth:
text.KJV: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.
text.NABRE: But now you must put them all away: anger, fury, malice, slander, and obscene language out of your mouths.
text.DRB: But now lay you also all away anger, indignation, malice, blasphemy, filthy talk out of your mouth.

text.NRSV: Do not lie to one another, seeing that you have stripped off the old self with its practices
text.Rotherham: Be not guilty of falsehood one to another: having stript off the old man, together with his practices,
text.KJV: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;
text.NABRE: Stop lying to one another, since you have taken off the old self with its practices
text.DRB: Lie not one to another: spoiling yourselves of the old man with his acts,

text.NRSV: and have clothed yourselves with the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of its creator.
text.Rotherham: And having put on the new—who is being moulded afresh unto personal knowledge, after the image of him that hath created him,—
text.KJV: And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:
text.NABRE: and have put on the new self, which is being renewed, for knowledge, in the image of its creator.
text.DRB: and doing on the new, him that is renewed unto knowledge, according to the image of him that created him.

text.NRSV: In that renewal there is no longer Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave and free; but Christ is all and in all!
text.Rotherham: Wherein there cannot be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, foreigner, Scythian, bond, free,—but, all things and in all, Christ:
text.KJV: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.
text.NABRE: Here there is not Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, slave, free; but Christ is all and in all.
text.DRB: Where there is not, Gentile & Jew, circumcision and prepuce, Barbarous and Scythian, bond and free: but all, and in all Christ.

text.NRSV: As God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience.
text.Rotherham: Put on, therefore, as men chosen of God, holy and beloved, tender affections of compassion, graciousness, lowliness of mind, meekness, long-suffering,
text.KJV: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;
text.NABRE: Put on then, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, heartfelt compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience,
text.DRB: Put ye on therefore as the elect of God, holy, and beloved, the bowels of mercy, benignity, humility, modesty, patience,

text.NRSV: Bear with one another and, if anyone has a complaint against another, forgive each other; just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also must forgive.
text.Rotherham: Bearing one with another, and in favour forgiving one another—if any, against any, have a complaint,—according as, the Lord, in favour forgave you, so also ye;
text.KJV: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.
text.NABRE: bearing with one another and forgiving one another, if one has a grievance against another; as the Lord has forgiven you, so must you also do.
text.DRB: supporting one another, & pardoning one another, if any have a quarrel against any man. As also our Lord hath pardoned us: so you also.

text.NRSV: Above all, clothe yourselves with love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony.
text.Rotherham: And, over all these things, love, which is a uniting-bond of completeness;
text.KJV: And above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness.
text.NABRE: And over all these put on love, that is, the bond of perfection.
text.DRB: But above all these things have charity, which is the band of perfection:

text.NRSV: And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in the one body. And be thankful.
text.Rotherham: And let, the peace of Christ, act as umpire in your hearts, unto which ye have been called in [one] body, and be thankful:
text.KJV: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.
text.NABRE: And let the peace of Christ control your hearts, the peace into which you were also called in one body. And be thankful.
text.DRB: and let the peace of Christ exult in your hearts, wherein also you are called in one body: and be thankful.

text.NRSV: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; teach and admonish one another in all wisdom; and with gratitude in your hearts sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs to God.
text.Rotherham: Let, the word of the Christ, dwell within you richly,—in all wisdom, teaching and admonishing one another, with psalms, hymns, spiritual songs, with gratitude, raising song with your hearts unto God:
text.KJV: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.
text.NABRE: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, as in all wisdom you teach and admonish one another, singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs with gratitude in your hearts to God.
text.DRB: Let the word of Christ dwell in you aboundantly, in all wisdom: teaching and admonishing your own selves, with psalms, hymns, and spiritual canticles, in grace singing in your hearts to God.

text.NRSV: And whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
text.Rotherham: And whatsoever ye may be doing, in word, or in work, all things, [do] in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks unto the Divine Father through him:—
text.KJV: And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.
text.NABRE: And whatever you do, in word or in deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
text.DRB: All whatsoever you do in word or in work, all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

text.NRSV: Wives, be subject to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Ye wives! be submitting yourselves unto your husbands, as is becoming in the Lord;
text.KJV: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Christian Family. Wives, be subordinate to your husbands, as is proper in the Lord.
text.DRB: Women be subject to your husbands, as it behoveth in our Lord.

text.NRSV: Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.
text.Rotherham: Ye husbands! be loving your wives, and be not embittered against them;
text.KJV: Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.
text.NABRE: Husbands, love your wives, and avoid any bitterness toward them.
text.DRB: Men, love your wives: and be not bitter toward them.

text.NRSV: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is your acceptable duty in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Ye children! be obedient unto your parents in all things, for, this, is, well pleasing, in the Lord;
text.KJV: Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is pleasing to the Lord.
text.DRB: Children obey your parents in all things: for that is well pleasing to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Fathers, do not provoke your children, or they may lose heart.
text.Rotherham: Ye fathers! be not irritating your children, lest they be disheartened;
text.KJV: Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged.
text.NABRE: Fathers, do not provoke your children, so they may not become discouraged.
text.DRB: Fathers, provoke not your children to indignation; that they become not discouraged.

text.NRSV: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything, not only while being watched and in order to please them, but wholeheartedly, fearing the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Ye servants! be obedient, in all things, unto them who, according to the flesh, are your masters,—not with eye-service, as man-pleasers, but with singleness of heart, revering the Lord,—
text.KJV: Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:
text.NABRE: Slaves and Masters. Slaves, obey your human masters in everything, not only when being watched, as currying favor, but in simplicity of heart, fearing the Lord.
text.DRB: Servants, obey in all things, your masters according to the flesh, not serving to the eye, as pleasing men, but in simplicity of heart, fearing God.

text.NRSV: Whatever your task, put yourselves into it, as done for the Lord and not for your masters,
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever ye may be doing, from the soul, be working at it, as unto the Lord, and not unto men,-
text.KJV: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;
text.NABRE: Whatever you do, do from the heart, as for the Lord and not for others,
text.DRB: Whatsoever you do, work it from the heart as to our Lord, and not to men:

text.NRSV: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward; you serve the Lord Christ.
text.Rotherham: Knowing that, from the Lord, ye shall duly receive the recompense of the inheritance,—unto the Lord Christ, are ye in service;
text.KJV: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.
text.NABRE: knowing that you will receive from the Lord the due payment of the inheritance; be slaves of the Lord Christ.
text.DRB: knowing that you shall receive of our Lord the retribution of inheritance. Serve our Lord Christ.

text.NRSV: For the wrongdoer will be paid back for whatever wrong has been done, and there is no partiality.
text.Rotherham: For, he that acteth unrighteously, shall get back what he had unrighteously done, and there is no respect of persons;
text.KJV: But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.
text.NABRE: For the wrongdoer will receive recompense for the wrong he committed, and there is no partiality.
text.DRB: For he that doeth injury, shall receive that which he hath done unjustly: and there is not acception of persons with God.

text.NRSV: Masters, treat your slaves justly and fairly, for you know that you also have a Master in heaven.
text.Rotherham: Ye masters! that which is just and equitable, unto your servants, be rendering, knowing that, ye also, have a Master in heaven.
text.KJV: Masters, give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Masters, treat your slaves justly and fairly, realizing that you too have a Master in heaven.
text.DRB: You Masters, that which is just and equal, do to your servants: knowing that you also have a Master in heaven.

text.NRSV: Devote yourselves to prayer, keeping alert in it with thanksgiving.
text.Rotherham: Unto prayer, be devoting yourselves, watching therein with thanksgiving:
text.KJV: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;
text.NABRE: Prayer and Apostolic Spirit. Persevere in prayer, being watchful in it with thanksgiving;
text.DRB: Be instant in prayer; watching in it in thanks-giving,

text.NRSV: At the same time pray for us as well that God will open to us a door for the word, that we may declare the mystery of Christ, for which I am in prison,
text.Rotherham: Praying, at the same time, for us also,—that, God, would open unto us a door for the word, so that we may speak the sacred secret of the Christ—for the sake of which also I am in bonds,
text.KJV: Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:
text.NABRE: at the same time, pray for us, too, that God may open a door to us for the word, to speak of the mystery of Christ, for which I am in prison,
text.DRB: praying withal for us also, that God may open unto us the door of speech to speak the mystery of Christ (for the which also I am bound)

text.NRSV: so that I may reveal it clearly, as I should.
text.Rotherham: That I may make it manifest as behoveth me to speak.
text.KJV: That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.
text.NABRE: that I may make it clear, as I must speak.
text.DRB: that I may manifest it, so as I ought to speak.

text.NRSV: Conduct yourselves wisely toward outsiders, making the most of the time.
text.Rotherham: In wisdom, be walking towards them who are without,—the opportunity, buying out for yourselves,
text.KJV: Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time.
text.NABRE: Conduct yourselves wisely toward outsiders, making the most of the opportunity.
text.DRB: Walk with wisdom toward them that be without; redeeming the time.

text.NRSV: Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how you ought to answer everyone.
text.Rotherham: Your discourse being always with benefit, with salt, seasoned,—that ye may know how it behoveth you, unto each one, to be making answer.
text.KJV: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.
text.NABRE: Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you know how you should respond to each one.
text.DRB: Your talk always, in grace let it be seasoned with salt: that you may know how you ought to answer every man.

text.NRSV: Tychicus will tell you all the news about me; he is a beloved brother, a faithful minister, and a fellow servant in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: All the things which relate unto myself, shall Tychicus make known unto you—the beloved brother and faithful minister and fellow-servant in the Lord,
text.KJV: All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:
text.NABRE: V. Conclusion Tychicus and Onesimus. Tychicus, my beloved brother, trustworthy minister, and fellow slave in the Lord, will tell you all the news of me.
text.DRB: The things that are about me, Tychicus, our dearest brother, and faithful minister, & fellow-servant in our Lord, will make you understand all,

text.NRSV: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, so that you may know how we are and that he may encourage your hearts;
text.Rotherham: Whom I have sent unto you, to this very end,—that ye may get to know the things which concern us, and he may encourage you hearts:
text.KJV: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;
text.NABRE: I am sending him to you for this very purpose, so that you may know about us and that he may encourage your hearts,
text.DRB: whom I have sent to you for this same purpose, that he may know the things that concern you, and may comfort your hearts,

text.NRSV: he is coming with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will tell you about everything here.
text.Rotherham: Together with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is from among you:—all things, unto you, will they make known, that [are taking place] here.
text.KJV: With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.
text.NABRE: together with Onesimus, a trustworthy and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will tell you about everything here.
text.DRB: with Onesimus, the most dear and faithful brother who is of you. All things that are done here, shall they do you to understand.

text.NRSV: Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you, as does Mark the cousin of Barnabas, concerning whom you have received instructions—if he comes to you, welcome him.
text.Rotherham: Aristarchus, my fellow-captive, saluteth you; and Mark, the first cousin of Barnabas,—concerning whom ye have received commands—if he come unto you, give him welcome;
text.KJV: Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister’s son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)
text.NABRE: From Paul’s Co-Workers. Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, sends you greetings, as does Mark the cousin of Barnabas (concerning whom you have received instructions; if he comes to you, receive him),
text.DRB: Aristarchus my fellow-prisoner saluteth you, & Mark the cousin-german of Barnabas (concerning whom you have received commandments, If he come to you, receive him)

text.NRSV: And Jesus who is called Justus greets you. These are the only ones of the circumcision among my co-workers for the kingdom of God, and they have been a comfort to me.
text.Rotherham: And Jesus, he that is called Justus,—they being of the circumcision; these only, [are my] fellow-workers unto the kingdom of God, men who have been, unto me, a comfort.
text.KJV: And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.
text.NABRE: and Jesus, who is called Justus, who are of the circumcision; these alone are my co-workers for the kingdom of God, and they have been a comfort to me.
text.DRB: and Jesus that is called Justus: who are of the Circumcision. These only are my coadjutors in the Kingdom of God: which have been a comfort to me.

text.NRSV: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ Jesus, greets you. He is always wrestling in his prayers on your behalf, so that you may stand mature and fully assured in everything that God wills.
text.Rotherham: Epaphras who is from among you, a servant of Christ Jesus, saluteth you,—at all times, contending in your behalf in his prayers, that ye may be caused to stand complete and fully assured in everything willed by God;
text.KJV: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.
text.NABRE: Epaphras sends you greetings; he is one of you, a slave of Christ [Jesus], always striving for you in his prayers so that you may be perfect and fully assured in all the will of God.
text.DRB: Epaphras saluteth you who is of you, the servant of Christ Jesus, always careful for you in prayers, that you may stand perfect and full in all the will of God.

text.NRSV: For I testify for him that he has worked hard for you and for those in Laodicea and in Hierapolis.
text.Rotherham: For I bear him witness, that he hath great toil in behalf of you, and them in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.
text.KJV: For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.
text.NABRE: For I can testify that he works very hard for you and for those in Laodicea and those in Hierapolis.
text.DRB: For I give him testimony that he hath much labour for you, & for them that be at Laodicia, and that are at Hierapolis.

text.NRSV: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas greet you.
text.Rotherham: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, salute you.
text.KJV: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you.
text.NABRE: Luke the beloved physician sends greetings, as does Demas.
text.DRB: Luke, the most dear physician, saluteth you; and Demas.

text.NRSV: Give my greetings to the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, and to Nympha and the church in her house.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye the brethren, in Laodicea, also Nymphas, and the assembly, which meeteth at her house.
text.KJV: Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.
text.NABRE: A Message for the Laodiceans. Give greetings to the brothers in Laodicea and to Nympha and to the church in her house.
text.DRB: Salute the brethren that are at Laodicia; and Nymphas, and the Church that is in his house.

text.NRSV: And when this letter has been read among you, have it read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and see that you read also the letter from Laodicea.
text.Rotherham: And, as soon as the epistle hath been read amongst you, cause that, in the assembly of Laodiceans also, it be read; and, that from Laodicea, that, ye also, read.
text.KJV: And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea.
text.NABRE: And when this letter is read before you, have it read also in the church of the Laodiceans, and you yourselves read the one from Laodicea.
text.DRB: And when the epistle shall be read with you, make that it be read also in the Church of the Laodicians: and that you read that which is of the Laodicians.

text.NRSV: And say to Archippus, “See that you complete the task that you have received in the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And say to Archippus: be taking heed unto the ministry which thou hast accepted in the Lord,—that, the same, thou fulfil.
text.KJV: And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.
text.NABRE: And tell Archippus, “See that you fulfill the ministry that you received in the Lord.”
text.DRB: And say to Archippus: See the ministry which thou hast received of our Lord, that thou fulfill it.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.
text.Rotherham: The salutation of me Paul, with my own hand:—keep in mind my bonds. Favour be with you!
text.KJV: The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen. Written from Rome to the Colossians by Tychicus and Onesimus.
text.NABRE: The greeting is in my own hand, Paul’s. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.
text.DRB: The salutation: with mine own hand, Paul's. Be mindful of my bands. Grace be with you. Amen.
